headline: Submit a serious implementation or enterprise brief
subheadline: Use this page when you need guided setup, scoped delivery, or a qualified deployment conversation before purchase.
stepsHeadline: The contact path is built to qualify work before follow-up starts

steps.title: Submit the brief
steps.description: Describe the outcome, deployment constraints, and timeline you want help with.

steps.title: Route the opportunity
steps.description: The lead is recorded with offer intent, source path, and stage inside the admin pipeline.

steps.title: Move into the right lane
steps.description: We use the same system to decide whether the next move is implementation scope, enterprise qualification, or a product path.

sections.title: Best for implementation work
sections.description: Use this form when you want the system configured, the public funnel sharpened, or the first delivery workflow built with you.

sections.title: Best for enterprise review
sections.description: Use this form when deployment, governance, hosting, or approval requirements need to be clarified before commercial scope.

sections.title: Not the fastest path for software-only buyers
sections.description: If you already know you want Operator OS, pricing is the faster route because it opens direct checkout.

leadForm.heading: Start the right follow-up path
leadForm.intro: Share enough detail to make implementation or deployment follow-up efficient and grounded in the real scope.

faq.question: Why use a brief instead of a generic contact form?
faq.answer: Because the point is to route the work into the correct operational lane, not just capture an email and lose context.
